{
  "name": "Currie-Rampersad Template Method",
  "seminal_paper": {
    "arxiv_id": "0905.1129",
    "doi": "10.1090/S0025-5718-2010-02407-X",
    "citation": "James D. Currie and Narad Rampersad, \"A proof of Dejean's conjecture,\" Mathematics of Computation 80(274):1063-1070, 2011.",
    "year": 2011
  },
  "home_domain": "combinatorics_on_words",
  "what_it_does": "The method certifies repetition-avoidance properties for infinite words produced by carefully chosen morphisms. It packages global repetition-threshold claims into finite checks involving encodings, kernel repetitions, and bounded forbidden configurations.",
  "input_requirements": [
    "an infinite word over a finite alphabet, typically generated by a uniform morphism or equivalent finite generator",
    "the target property expressed as avoidance of fractional powers or finite forbidden templates in that word",
    "a Pansiot-style encoding or comparable finite-state encoding preserving periods and exponents",
    "an algebraic condition on the morphism, such as compatibility with a finite permutation semigroup",
    "a bounded exhaustive check of candidate bad factors or kernel repetitions"
  ],
  "output_signature": "a certified infinite word avoiding the specified repetitions or templates, usually reducing the global avoidance claim to a finite verification certificate",
  "input_match": [
    {
      "requirement": "an infinite word over a finite alphabet, typically generated by a uniform morphism or equivalent finite generator",
      "match": "PARTIAL",
      "evidence": "Prior slot A3 supplies a gap-sequence word viewpoint, but the gap alphabet is not finite and no morphic generator is identified."
    },
    {
      "requirement": "the target property expressed as avoidance of fractional powers or finite forbidden templates in that word",
      "match": "PARTIAL",
      "evidence": "Prior slot A3 maps a consecutive 3-term AP to an adjacent equal-gap pattern, but only as a local square condition."
    },
    {
      "requirement": "a Pansiot-style encoding or comparable finite-state encoding preserving periods and exponents",
      "match": "NO",
      "evidence": "no match"
    },
    {
      "requirement": "an algebraic condition on the morphism, such as compatibility with a finite permutation semigroup",
      "match": "NO",
      "evidence": "no match"
    },
    {
      "requirement": "a bounded exhaustive check of candidate bad factors or kernel repetitions",
      "match": "NO",
      "evidence": "no match"
    }
  ],
  "known_obstructions": [
    "Powerful numbers are defined by multiplicative prime-valuation conditions rather than by a morphic fixed point.",
    "The natural gap sequence has unbounded integer symbols, unlike the finite alphabets used in the template method.",
    "The desired conclusion is finiteness of occurrences, not construction of an infinite object avoiding all forbidden repetitions.",
    "No Pansiot encoding, kernel-repetition structure, or finite verification bound is visible from the stated Lean problem."
  ],
  "fit_score": 0.2,
  "source_analog_id": "A3"
}
